reconciled.rationale_completion: Two teachers within 0.2s of each other — essentially perfect agreement on completion time.

phase_timings.rationale: Adopting GPT-4o's phase split since Claude only provided coarse phase data for this video. Note: GPT-4o flags the second throw at 58s as the dominant time sink, breaking the systematic 'GPT-4o inflates third_throw' pattern seen in post2/V7/V8.

training_signal.note: Fourth dual-teacher comparison and the closest agreement yet. ΔFLS = 0.3 points, completion time within 0.2s. Both teachers see complete closure, surgeon's knot, hand alternation, low penalties, and identify the same coaching priority (knot inefficiency, not technical failure). This is a near-perfect calibration point for the consensus pipeline.
training_signal.cross_comparison_finding: GPT-4o's phase attribution for post_practice DOES NOT follow the third-throw-inflation pattern seen in post2/V7/V8 — here the second throw at 58s is the bottleneck. This suggests the third-throw inflation may only kick in when there is genuine 'extra reinforcing manipulation' to attribute. When the bottleneck is unambiguously elsewhere, GPT-4o assigns it correctly.

notes: Fourth teacher comparison. Score deltas across the four comparisons now: post_practice 0.3, postV7 3.5, post2 4.9, postV8 17.5. The pattern: clean, well-completed runs converge tightly; the only large delta is on a fatigue-affected run with disputed gap visibility. This is exactly the behavior the teacher-critique-student architecture predicts and benefits from.
